seoTitle: Kensington Park vs Kent Town — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Kensington Park and Kent Town. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Kensington Park vs Kent Town: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Kensington Park and Kent Town.
heading: Kensington Park vs Kent Town — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Kensington Park, South Australia and Kent Town, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Kent Town has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Kensington Park (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Kensington Park or Kent Town safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Kensington Park has a flood risk score of 49/100, while Kent Town scores 21/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Kensington Park scores 19/100 for bushfire risk. Kent Town scores 19/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Kensington Park or Kent Town?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Kent Town has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
